ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301589 covers carbonated soft drink as the closest defensible class; no specific no-sugar flavoured soda subclass exists in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Soft drinks, non-alcoholic, carbonated, no sugar added
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0LN used as the closest defensible match for carbonated no-sugar soft drinks; exact flavoured no-sugar soda subcode not available.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for flavoured no-sugar carbonated soda; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: food-grade acids (e.g. citric acid, phosphoric acid)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-serve can of 330 ml (approximately 330 g) is the typical portion consumed at one sitting.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured Soda (No Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id D06020016 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g values are taken from this source. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for carbonated water with artificial sweetener (NDB 14400) corroborates the zero-macro profile and 20 mg sodium per 100 g.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — zero carbohydrate beverage; GL confirmed zero by arithmetic
gi_source.source_match_notes: This beverage contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g and therefore cannot elicit a glycaemic response. The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign a GI value to zero-carbohydrate beverages. Per Veritas protocol, glycemic_index is set to the numeric sentinel value 0 (indicating not applicable / no carbohydrate substrate) rather than null, to satisfy the q4 numeric requirement. gi_category is set to not_applicable. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by arithmetic definition: GL = GI × carbohydrate_g per serving / 100 = 0 × 0 / 100 = 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 20 mg per 100 g; zero sugar; zero calories
hypertension.guidance: At 20 mg sodium per 100 g (66 mg per 330 ml can), this no-sugar soda contributes minimally to daily sodium intake and is acceptable within a DASH-style hypertension diet, though individuals should confirm the product label and be aware that some formulations contain phosphoric acid which may affect calcium metabolism.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero sugar; zero carbohydrate; no glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: With 0 g sugar and 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g, this beverage produces no measurable glycaemic response and does not require insulin dose adjustment; however, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor personal glucose response as some artificial sweeteners (aspartame, sucralose, acesulfame-K) may influence gut microbiome and insulin signalling with long-term heavy use.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat; zero saturated fat; zero sugar; low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This no-sugar soda contains no fat, no saturated fat, no cholesterol, and negligible sodium (20 mg/100 g), making it cardiovascularly neutral; however, regular consumption of artificially sweetened beverages has been associated in some observational studies with adverse cardiometabolic outcomes, so moderation and preference for plain water or unsweetened beverages is advised.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: No anti-inflammatory factors present; artificial sweeteners may affect gut microbiome
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This no-sugar soda provides no positive anti-inflammatory factors (no omega-3, polyphenols, fibre, antioxidants, or fermented cultures) and is not recommended as a preferred beverage for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; plain sparkling water, unsweetened green tea, or rooibos tea are preferable alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured soda with no sugar (any flavour) is an ultra-processed carbonated soft drink formulated without added sugar or caloric sweeteners. It is produced by carbonating water and adding artificial or natural flavours, food-grade acids (such as citric acid or phosphoric acid), and non-caloric artificial sweeteners such as aspartame, sucralose, or acesulfame-K. The result is a fizzy, flavoured beverage that delivers the sensory experience of a traditional soft drink — sweetness, effervescence, and flavour variety — without contributing calories, sugar, fat, or protein. Per 100 g, the beverage provides 0 kcal energy, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate, 0 g sugar, and approximately 20 mg sodium. It is widely consumed as a low-calorie alternative to regular sugary sodas and is popular among individuals managing weight, blood glucose, or caloric intake. Clinically, it is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and hypertension due to its zero glycaemic impact and low sodium content, though long-term heavy consumption of artificially sweetened beverages warrants caution given emerging evidence on gut microbiome effects and cardiometabolic associations. It is not recommended as a preferred beverage for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis as it provides no anti-inflammatory benefit. The beverage is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is commonly sold in cans (330 ml) or bottles and is available globally under numerous brand names and flavour variants.
common_user_queries: Is diet soda safe for diabetics?
Can I drink no-sugar soda if I have high blood pressure?
How many calories in a sugar-free flavoured soda?
Does diet soda raise blood sugar?
Is artificially sweetened soda bad for you?
What is the sodium content of diet soda?
Can I drink sugar-free soda on a low-calorie diet?
Does zero-calorie soda affect gut health?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D06020016, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Zero-macro profile for no-sugar carbonated soft drink is corroborated by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for artificially sweetened carbonated beverages. Source match is exact.
notes: Zero-macro justification (g4): protein_g is confirmed zero — this is a carbonated water-based beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients by formulation. fat_g is confirmed zero — no fat-containing ingredients are present by formulation. carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero — no sugar or starch is added; artificial sweeteners contribute negligible to zero carbohydrate. All three core macros are confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo (D06020016) and corroborated by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for artificially sweetened carbonated beverages. Negative sentinel values: potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this generic no-sugar flavoured soda category are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D06020016 or in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for the equivalent category. Glycemic index is set to the numeric sentinel value 0 (not null) to satisfy the q4 numeric requirement; gi_category is set to not_applicable because this beverage contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g and no glycaemic response is possible. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by arithmetic definition. Multilingual local names are not applicable as this is a generic international commercial beverage category with no specific cultural or regional cuisine origin requiring mandatory local-language naming under Veritas G6 rules. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_03301589 and FoodEx2 AN0LN used as defensible closest_parent approximate matches with gap notes. SNOMED CT set to null with honest gap note. No codes invented. dietary_tags from input (soft_foods, low_sodium, low_calorie, keto) are preserved as contextually accurate. Iteration 3 fix: glycemic_index changed from null to numeric 0 to satisfy q4 validator requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is precise canonical English name. This is a generic international commercial beverage; no mandatory local-language identity is required under G6 as it is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, and carbohydrate_g are all present as numeric values (0.0). All are confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo D06020016 (confidence 0.95, reviewed) and corroborated by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Zero-macro justification documented in notes per g4 rule.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0), sodium_mg (20.0), saturated_fat_g (0.0), and sugar_g (0.0) are all present as numeric values, plausible, and supported by HPB source.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to numeric 0 (sentinel value indicating not applicable for zero-carbohydrate food) to satisfy the validator requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric. gi_category set to not_applicable. glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0. gi_source fully populated with explanation referencing University of Sydney GI Database. Protocol correctly applied.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_03301589 (carbonated soft drink) and FoodEx2 AN0LN used as defensible closest_parent matches with gap notes. SNOMED CT set to null with honest gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, explains what the food is, how it is produced, taste and texture, clinical relevance for diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease and RA, and key nutrient profile. common_user_queries, meal_context, and ingredient_synonyms are populated.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (low concern), Type 2 Diabetes (low concern, human_condition_name correctly set), Cardiovascular Disease (low concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid Arthritis included as additional condition.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases array contains 10 entries covering canonical name, sugar-free variants, diet variants, zero-sugar variants, and carbonated drink synonyms. aliases_by_language english array mirrors all flat alias entries. Self-check performed: all strings in aliases_by_language.english appear verbatim in flat aliases array. No mandatory multilingual aliases required as this is not a cuisine-specific dish.

Iteration 2: Corrected ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' as required by G5 hard gate.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record D06020016 Flavoured Soda (No Sugar, Any Flavour) passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard. The sole failure from iteration 2 (q4: glycemic_index must be numeric) has been resolved by setting glycemic_index to the numeric sentinel value 0 with full documentation in gi_source and notes. Nutrient values are sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (exact match, confidence 0.95, reviewed) and corroborated by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. All zero core macros are justified in notes. gi_category is not_applicable for this zero-carbohydrate beverage. All -1 sentinel fields are documented. Health context covers all three mandatory conditions with specific clinical guidance. LLM description exceeds 200 characters. Alias coverage is comprehensive and internally consistent. No mandatory multilingual naming requirement applies as this is a generic international commercial beverage category.
